tasks.title: Bootstrap VitePress documentation site
tasks.intent: Create the doc site skeleton with VitePress, including config, homepage, and navigation.
tasks.transformation: Add VitePress dependency, create docs/ directory with config, homepage, and sidebar. Add npm script `docs:dev` and `docs:build`.
tasks.evidence_requirement: Running `npm run docs:dev` serves the site locally without errors.
tasks.review_predicate: A reviewer can clone the repo, run `npm install && npm run docs:dev`, and see the homepage.
tasks.residual_handling: If VitePress fails to install or run on Node 18+, create a residual to evaluate alternative static site generators.

tasks.title: Document the three main widgets
tasks.intent: Write usage documentation for Button, Card, and Modal with code examples.
tasks.transformation: Create three MDX pages with installation, props tables, and live code examples for Button, Card, and Modal.
tasks.evidence_requirement: Each page contains at least one runnable code example.
tasks.review_predicate: A new developer can read the three pages and understand how to render each widget without reading source code.
tasks.residual_handling: If widget APIs are undocumented or ambiguous, create a residual to audit the public API surface.

tasks.title: Add CI validation for doc examples
tasks.intent: Prevent documentation drift by validating code examples in CI.
tasks.transformation: Add a GitHub Actions workflow that extracts code examples from docs/ and runs them against the built library.
tasks.evidence_requirement: A deliberate invalid example in a test PR fails the CI check.
tasks.review_predicate: Invalid examples are caught; valid examples pass; the workflow completes in < 2 minutes.
tasks.residual_handling: If extraction or validation is too complex, create a residual to evaluate external doc-test tools.

tasks.title: Deploy to GitHub Pages
tasks.intent: Make the documentation site publicly accessible.
tasks.transformation: Add a GitHub Actions workflow that builds the docs on every push to main and deploys to GitHub Pages.
output_artifact: Live site URL in repo settings
tasks.evidence_requirement: Pushing to main triggers a successful deploy; the site is reachable at the published URL.
tasks.review_predicate: The site loads without errors; the homepage displays correctly.
tasks.residual_handling: If GitHub Pages fails or is unavailable, create a residual to evaluate Netlify or Vercel.
